summary: Explosive NFA→Lovelight jam, soaring Easy Wind, tight set flow
blurb: The set erupts with a blistering Not Fade Away → Turn On Your Lovelight segue and a soaring Easy Wind. Jerry’s guitar tone is immaculate while Phil’s thumping bass drives the band forward. Highlights include China Cat Sunflower > I Know You Rider > High Time and Pigpen’s vocal fireworks on Lovelight.
review: On February 27, 1970 at the Family Dog on The Great Highway, the Dead served up one of their early‑70s high‑water marks. From the opening Cold Rain And Snow with its unique intro to the raucous Me And My Uncle, the band never lost momentum. The centerpiece was an explosive Not Fade Away → Turn On Your Lovelight marathon that left the audience chanting, while Jerry Garcia’s guitar was described by multiple listeners as “finest playing ever” with a perfect tone. Phil Lesh’s bass was lauded as “phenomenal – thumping, unbelievable harmony,” anchoring the group through tight jams like China Cat Sunflower → I Know You Rider → High Time and the soaring Easy Wind that floated above the mix. Pigpen shone on Lovelight, delivering vocal raps and harmonies that fans called peak Pigpen moments. The set closed with a second Lovelight encore that sustained the high‑energy flow, making this show a must‑listen for anyone chasing early‑70s Dead firepower.

rating_calculation.sentiment_rationale: Both recordings are overwhelmingly positive, praising the NFA→Lovelight segue, Jerry’s tone, Phil’s bass, and Pigpen’s vocals. No negative comments about performance appear, so a strong positive adjustment is applied.

best_recording.reason: Crisp soundboard capture with impeccable Charlie Miller transfer; highlights the NFA→Lovelight jam and Easy Wind with clear instrument separation.

key_highlights: Explosive Not Fade Away → Turn On Your Lovelight marathon
Tight China Cat Sunflower > I Know You Rider > High Time sequence
Pigpen’s vocal raps and harmonies on Lovelight

band_performance.Jerry: His guitar work is described as his finest playing ever with a perfect tone throughout the set.
band_performance.Phil: Phenomenal thumping bass and unbelievable harmony that drives the band’s energy.
band_performance.Bob: Melodic vocal on “Me And My Uncle” stands out, plus strong backing vocals with Pigpen on Lovelight.
band_performance.Pigpen: Peak Pigpen moments: vocal raps and harmonies on “Turn On Your Lovelight” add fire to the jam.
